summary: 缅因州民主党初选中，丑闻缠身的格雷厄姆·普拉特纳以约75%得票率击败州长珍妮特·米尔斯，将挑战共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯。普拉特纳需争取米尔斯支持者，而柯林斯在独立选民中仍有优势。其他新闻：美国对伊朗发动导弹袭击；OpenAI申请IPO；埃普斯坦档案听证会；科罗拉多州长候选人维克多·马克思争议言论。

vocabulary.en: to continue to support or choose someone or something
vocabulary.excerpt: Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner.
vocabulary.example_cn: 缅因州民主党人投票决定继续支持格雷厄姆·普拉特纳。

vocabulary.cn: 翻转（指政党在选举中赢得原本由对手控制的席位）
vocabulary.en: to win a seat or office that was previously held by the opposing party
vocabulary.excerpt: Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall?
vocabulary.example_cn: 这会削弱该党在今年秋天翻转参议院的机会吗？

vocabulary.en: to consider something carefully, especially by comparing different factors
vocabulary.excerpt: that's the situation that we have to weigh.
vocabulary.example_cn: 那是我们必须权衡的情况。

vocabulary.en: to cause something to be under pressure or tension
vocabulary.excerpt: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran?
vocabulary.example_cn: 紧张的停火能否持续足够长的时间，让美国兑现与伊朗的长期承诺协议？

vocabulary.en: to fulfill a promise or expectation; to provide something promised
vocabulary.excerpt: for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran.
vocabulary.example_cn: 让美国兑现与伊朗的长期承诺协议。

vocabulary.cn: 领先者；最有希望获胜者
vocabulary.en: the person or thing that is most likely to win a competition or election
vocabulary.excerpt: Meet the frontrunner in the Republican race for governor of Colorado.
vocabulary.example_cn: 认识一下科罗拉多州共和党州长竞选中的领先者。

vocabulary.cn: 泡沫（指资产价格脱离基本面快速上涨）
vocabulary.en: a situation in which the price of an asset rises far above its real value, often followed by a sudden collapse
vocabulary.excerpt: signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: 向我表明人工智能泡沫即将破裂。

vocabulary.en: to burst or break open suddenly, often with a sound
vocabulary.excerpt: the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: 人工智能泡沫即将破裂。

vocabulary.cn: 获得（提名/资格）；成功晋级
vocabulary.en: to secure a nomination or qualification, especially in politics
vocabulary.excerpt: Graham Platner punches his ticket.
vocabulary.example_cn: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳获得了提名。

vocabulary.en: to finally win or achieve something, especially after a struggle
vocabulary.excerpt: Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent.
vocabulary.example_cn: 在锁定提名后不久，他将注意力转向了他的新对手。

vocabulary.en: lacking courage or determination; weak-willed
vocabulary.excerpt: she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她变得和她现在服务的建制派一样懦弱和腐败。

vocabulary.en: the group of people who have power and influence in a society or organization, often seen as conservative or resistant to change
vocabulary.excerpt: as the establishment she now serves.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她所服务的建制派。

vocabulary.cn: 独立的声音（指不依附政党、独立表达意见的人）
vocabulary.en: a person or entity that expresses opinions independently, not aligned with a particular party or group
vocabulary.excerpt: If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
vocabulary.example_cn: 如果你是一个独立的声音，为什么你95%的时间都和唐纳德·特朗普投票一致？

vocabulary.word: have a dog in the fight
vocabulary.cn: 在竞争中有利益关系；参与其中
vocabulary.en: to have a personal stake or interest in a particular situation or conflict
vocabulary.excerpt: you guys had a dog in the fight in this race.
vocabulary.example_cn: 你们在这场竞选中有利害关系。

vocabulary.en: at risk; something that could be lost or gained
vocabulary.excerpt: what's at stake here.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这里利害攸关的是什么。

vocabulary.en: to put something at risk of being lost or harmed
vocabulary.excerpt: Maybe this has jeopardized it.
vocabulary.example_cn: 也许这已经危及到了它。

vocabulary.cn: 压倒性胜利；一边倒的选举
vocabulary.en: an easy or decisive victory, especially in an election or sports event
vocabulary.excerpt: A lot of times it can be a blowout, right?
vocabulary.example_cn: 很多时候它可能是一场压倒性胜利，对吧？

vocabulary.en: fundamentally; in its most important aspects
vocabulary.excerpt: we're saying the same thing in essence, right?
vocabulary.example_cn: 我们本质上说的是同一件事，对吧？

vocabulary.en: difficult to defeat or overcome
vocabulary.excerpt: Susan Collins is tough to beat.
vocabulary.example_cn: 苏珊·柯林斯很难被击败。

vocabulary.cn: 苏珊·柯林斯的弱化版（指立场相似但更温和的候选人）
vocabulary.en: a candidate who is similar to Susan Collins but less extreme or less effective
vocabulary.excerpt: you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light.
vocabulary.example_cn: 你不可能通过选出一个苏珊·柯林斯的弱化版来赢得这个席位。

vocabulary.cn: 激励你的基本盘（核心支持者）
vocabulary.en: to motivate and rally the core supporters of a political party or candidate
vocabulary.excerpt: You are going to beat her by energizing your base.
vocabulary.example_cn: 你要通过激励你的基本盘来击败她。

vocabulary.cn: 中期选举（美国总统任期中间举行的国会选举）
vocabulary.en: an election held in the middle of a president's term, typically for Congress and state offices
vocabulary.excerpt: particularly in a midterm election.
vocabulary.example_cn: 尤其是在中期选举中。

vocabulary.en: a direct vote by the electorate on a specific proposal or issue
vocabulary.excerpt: this election has got to be a referendum on Susan Collins.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这次选举必须是对苏珊·柯林斯的公投。

vocabulary.en: to require someone to explain their actions and accept responsibility
vocabulary.excerpt: her refusal to hold Donald Trump accountable.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她拒绝追究唐纳德·特朗普的责任。

vocabulary.en: consecutively; one after another
vocabulary.excerpt: I want to play two clips back-to-back.
vocabulary.example_cn: 我想连续播放两段剪辑。

vocabulary.cn: 强有力的支持；明确的背书
vocabulary.en: a strong and enthusiastic statement of support
vocabulary.excerpt: these are not ringing endorsements.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这些都不是强有力的支持。

vocabulary.en: without her consent or desire
vocabulary.excerpt: who said she voted for Susan Collins, against her will.
vocabulary.example_cn: 她说她违背自己的意愿投票给了苏珊·柯林斯。

vocabulary.en: the moral and ethical qualities of a person
vocabulary.excerpt: It's really about the character quality.
vocabulary.example_cn: 这实际上是关于品格素质。

vocabulary.en: a close and strong relationship
vocabulary.excerpt: Collins and Trump, that's not a tight bond.
vocabulary.example_cn: 柯林斯和特朗普，关系并不紧密。

vocabulary.en: a mechanism that initiates a process or event
vocabulary.excerpt: The 60 days is a trigger.
vocabulary.example_cn: 60天是一个触发点。

vocabulary.en: formal consent or authorization by the U.S. Congress
vocabulary.excerpt: the president has to obtain congressional approval.
vocabulary.example_cn: 总统必须获得国会批准。

vocabulary.en: found guilty of a crime by a court of law
vocabulary.excerpt: individuals who were convicted of violence against police officers.
vocabulary.example_cn: 被判犯有暴力袭击警察罪的人。

vocabulary.en: the act of paying back money spent or lost
vocabulary.excerpt: should be entitled to reimbursement for their legal fees.
vocabulary.example_cn: 应该有权报销他们的法律费用。

vocabulary.en: charges for legal services provided by a lawyer or law firm
vocabulary.excerpt: reimbursement for their legal fees.
vocabulary.example_cn: 报销他们的法律费用。

vocabulary.cn: 削减（通常指预算或开支）
vocabulary.en: reductions in spending, especially in government budgets
vocabulary.excerpt: Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid.
vocabulary.example_cn: 担心医疗补助的削减。

vocabulary.cn: 医疗补助（美国联邦和州政府为低收入人群提供的医疗保险）
vocabulary.en: a U.S. government program that provides health insurance to low-income individuals and families
vocabulary.excerpt: Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid.
vocabulary.example_cn: 担心医疗补助的削减。

vocabulary.cn: 执法激增；加强执法行动
vocabulary.en: a sudden increase in law enforcement activities, often targeting specific issues
vocabulary.excerpt: a pause on the enforcement surge in both Maine and in Minne...
vocabulary.example_cn: 暂停缅因州和明尼苏达州的执法激增行动。

vocabulary.en: a weapon that is thrown or fired through the air, especially one that is guided
vocabulary.excerpt: Missiles light up the sky in the Middle East again.
vocabulary.example_cn: 导弹再次照亮中东的天空。

vocabulary.en: a temporary agreement to stop fighting
vocabulary.excerpt: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough?
vocabulary.example_cn: 紧张的停火能否持续足够长的时间？

vocabulary.cn: 爱泼斯坦文件（指与已故性犯罪者杰弗里·爱泼斯坦相关的案件文件）
vocabulary.en: documents related to the case of Jeffrey Epstein, a convicted sex offender
vocabulary.excerpt: a big day for the Epstein files.
vocabulary.example_cn: 爱泼斯坦文件的重要日子。

vocabulary.en: a person who has been convicted of a sexual crime
vocabulary.excerpt: the late convicted sex offender.
vocabulary.example_cn: 已故的已定罪性犯罪者。

vocabulary.cn: 州长的；与州长选举有关的
vocabulary.en: relating to a governor or the election of a governor
vocabulary.excerpt: Colorado gubernatorial candidate.
vocabulary.example_cn: 科罗拉多州州长候选人。

vocabulary.en: Initial Public Offering; the first sale of a company's shares to the public
vocabulary.excerpt: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs.
vocabulary.example_cn: OpenAI和Anthropic计划进行首次公开募股。

vocabulary.en: a situation where an economic bubble is about to burst
vocabulary.excerpt: the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: 人工智能泡沫即将破裂。

vocabulary.en: to indicate or show something, often indirectly
vocabulary.excerpt: signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
vocabulary.example_cn: 向我表明人工智能泡沫即将破裂。

vocabulary.en: a helicopter that has been shot down or forced to crash
vocabulary.excerpt: U.S. Hits Iran after Helicopter Downed.
vocabulary.example_cn: 直升机被击落后美国打击伊朗。

vocabulary.en: to attack or strike, especially militarily
vocabulary.excerpt: U.S. Hits Iran after Helicopter Downed.
vocabulary.example_cn: 直升机被击落后美国打击伊朗。

vocabulary.en: a person who makes laws; a member of a legislature
vocabulary.excerpt: What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links?
vocabulary.example_cn: 比尔·盖茨将告诉立法者关于他与爱泼斯坦的联系什么？

vocabulary.en: connections or relationships between people or things
vocabulary.excerpt: his links to the late convicted sex offender.
vocabulary.example_cn: 他与已故的已定罪性犯罪者的联系。

vocabulary.en: to have committed murder when one was a child
vocabulary.excerpt: a candidate who also claims he killed someone as a child.
vocabulary.example_cn: 一位还声称自己小时候杀过人的候选人。

sentences.en: Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner.
sentences.cn: 缅因州的民主党人投票支持格雷厄姆·普拉特纳。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Democrats in Maine）+ 谓语（vote）+ 不定式短语（to stick with Graham Platner）作宾语。
sentences.analysis: stick with 意为“坚持支持”，是新闻常用短语。

sentences.en: Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall?
sentences.cn: 这会让该党失去今年秋天翻转参议院的机会吗？
sentences.structure: 疑问句：情态动词（Could）+ 主语（that）+ 谓语（cost）+ 间接宾语（the party）+ 直接宾语（a shot at flipping the Senate this fall）。
sentences.analysis: cost sb. a shot at sth. 意为“使某人失去做某事的机会”；flip the Senate 指“翻转参议院（控制权）”，是政治新闻常用表达。

sentences.en: If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take health care away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
sentences.cn: 如果选票上的选择是在那个（指普拉特纳的争议行为）和一位投票剥夺数百万美国人医保的参议员之间，那就是我们必须权衡的情况。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（If...）+ 主句（that's the situation）+ 定语从句（that we have to weigh）。
sentences.analysis: weigh 意为“权衡”，是抽象动词；take health care away from 是新闻中常见的负面政策描述。

sentences.en: Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long-promised deal with Iran?
sentences.cn: 紧张的停火协议能否持续足够长的时间，让美国兑现与伊朗长期承诺的协议？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句：Will + 主语（the strained ceasefire）+ 谓语（last）+ 状语（long enough）+ 不定式短语（for the U.S. to deliver...）。
sentences.analysis: strained ceasefire 意为“紧张的停火”；long-promised 是复合形容词，意为“长期承诺的”。

sentences.en: What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links to the late convicted sex offender.
sentences.cn: 比尔·盖茨将就他与已故被定罪性犯罪者的关联向立法者说些什么。
sentences.structure: 特殊疑问句：What + 助动词（will）+ 主语（Bill Gates）+ 谓语（tell）+ 间接宾语（lawmakers）+ 介词短语（about his links...）。
sentences.analysis: the late convicted sex offender 指“已故被定罪的性犯罪者”，late 意为“已故的”，convicted 是过去分词作定语。

sentences.en: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult?
sentences.cn: 你认为你成年后杀过人吗？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句（Do you think）+ 宾语从句（that you've killed people as an adult）。
sentences.analysis: as an adult 意为“作为成年人”，与后文“as a child”形成对比。

sentences.en: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop.
sentences.cn: OpenAI 和 Anthropic 计划在今年年底前进行首次公开募股，这向我表明人工智能泡沫即将破裂。
sentences.structure: 主句（OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs...）+ 现在分词短语（signaling to me that...）作结果状语，其中包含宾语从句（that the A.I. bubble is about to pop）。
sentences.analysis: IPO 是 Initial Public Offering 的缩写；signal to sb. that... 意为“向某人表明……”；be about to pop 意为“即将破裂”。

sentences.en: To any of those who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned, it is my job to earn your trust, faith, and support.
sentences.cn: 对于任何感到失望、沮丧或幻想破灭的人，我的工作是赢得你们的信任、信念和支持。
sentences.structure: 介词短语（To any of those...）作状语，其中包含定语从句（who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned）；主句（it is my job to earn...）。
sentences.analysis: let down 意为“失望”；disillusioned 意为“幻想破灭”；it is my job to do sth. 是强调结构的常见表达。

sentences.en: And I will spend every day of this campaign.
sentences.cn: 我将花费这场竞选的每一天。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（I）+ 谓语（will spend）+ 宾语（every day of this campaign）。
sentences.analysis: spend 后接时间名词，表示“花费时间”。

sentences.en: And if I have the privilege every day in the United States Senate doing exactly that.
sentences.cn: 如果我有幸每天在美国参议院做同样的事。
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（if I have the privilege...），其中包含现在分词短语（doing exactly that）作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: have the privilege doing sth. 意为“有幸做某事”；the United States Senate 是专有名词。

sentences.en: Graham Platner punches his ticket.
sentences.cn: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳获得了提名。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Graham Platner）+ 谓语（punches）+ 宾语（his ticket）。
sentences.analysis: punch one's ticket 是习语，意为“获得（政治）提名或资格”。

sentences.en: But now he's got to convince voters to choose him over a five-term senator.
sentences.cn: 但现在他必须说服选民选择他而不是一位连任五届的参议员。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（he）+ 谓语（'s got to convince）+ 宾语（voters）+ 不定式短语（to choose him over...）。
sentences.analysis: convince sb. to do sth. 意为“说服某人做某事”；choose A over B 意为“选择A而不是B”；five-term 是复合形容词，意为“连任五届的”。

sentences.en: Democrat Graham Platner, despite all the scandals, earned about three-fourths of primary voters' support over Governor Janet Mills.
sentences.cn: 民主党人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳，尽管有所有丑闻，还是赢得了大约四分之三初选选民的支持，超过了州长珍妮特·米尔斯。
sentences.structure: 主语（Democrat Graham Platner）+ 插入语（despite all the scandals）+ 谓语（earned）+ 宾语（about three-fourths of primary voters' support）+ 状语（over Governor Janet Mills）。
sentences.analysis: despite 表示让步；three-fourths 是分数表达；over 在此表示“超过”。

sentences.en: Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent, Republican Senator Susan Collins.
sentences.cn: 在获得提名后不久，他就将注意力转向了他的新对手，共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯。
sentences.structure: 时间状语（Moments after clinching the nomination）+ 主句（he turned his attention to...），其中宾语（his new opponent）后跟同位语（Republican Senator Susan Collins）。
sentences.analysis: clinch the nomination 意为“获得提名”；turn one's attention to 意为“将注意力转向”。

sentences.en: Susan Collins may have started her career decades ago in Washington with good intentions, but she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves.
sentences.cn: 苏珊·柯林斯可能几十年前在华盛顿以良好的意图开始了她的职业生涯，但她已经变得和她现在所服务的建制派一样软弱和腐败。
sentences.structure: 并列句：第一分句（Susan Collins may have started...）+ but 连接第二分句（she has become...），其中包含比较结构（as...as...）和定语从句（she now serves）。
sentences.analysis: may have started 表示对过去的推测；spineless 意为“没有骨气的”；the establishment 指“建制派”。

sentences.en: If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
sentences.cn: 如果你是一个独立的声音，为什么你95%的时间都和唐纳德·特朗普投票一致？
sentences.structure: 条件状语从句（If you are...）+ 主句（why do you vote...）。
sentences.analysis: independent voice 意为“独立的声音”；vote with sb. 意为“与某人投相同票”。

sentences.en: Platner has a lot of work to do to get Mills voters on his side.
sentences.cn: 普拉特纳有很多工作要做，以争取米尔斯选民的支持。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（Platner）+ 谓语（has）+ 宾语（a lot of work）+ 不定式短语（to do to get...）作定语和目的状语。
sentences.analysis: get sb. on one's side 意为“争取某人支持”。

sentences.en: I couldn't honestly support Platner, even though some of the things that he says are really good.
sentences.cn: 我无法诚实地支持普拉特纳，尽管他说的一些话确实很好。
sentences.structure: 主句（I couldn't honestly support Platner）+ 让步状语从句（even though some of the things...），其中包含定语从句（that he says）。
sentences.analysis: honestly 是副词，修饰 support；even though 引导让步状语从句。

sentences.en: I don't think he's ready to be a senator.
sentences.cn: 我认为他还没有准备好当参议员。
sentences.structure: 主句（I don't think）+ 宾语从句（he's ready to be a senator）。
sentences.analysis: 否定转移：主句否定，从句肯定。

sentences.en: I'm leaning towards Collins unless the guy shows something else, but I just -- he's not there yet.
sentences.cn: 我倾向于柯林斯，除非那家伙表现出其他东西，但我只是——他还没到那个程度。
sentences.structure: 并列句：第一分句（I'm leaning towards Collins unless...）+ but 连接第二分句（I just -- he's not there yet）。
sentences.analysis: lean towards 意为“倾向于”；unless 引导条件状语从句；he's not there yet 是口语表达，意为“他还没达到标准”。

sentences.en: And so, what will you do in November if its Platner versus Collins?
sentences.cn: 那么，如果十一月是普拉特纳对阵柯林斯，你会怎么做？
sentences.structure: 特殊疑问句（what will you do...）+ 条件状语从句（if its Platner versus Collins）。
sentences.analysis: its 应为 it's 的笔误；versus 意为“对阵”。

sentences.en: I'm a Democrat, but I voted for Susan Collins several times, because I think that she does care about the state of Maine.
sentences.cn: 我是民主党人，但我多次投票给苏珊·柯林斯，因为我认为她确实关心缅因州。
sentences.structure: 并列句：第一分句（I'm a Democrat）+ but 连接第二分句（I voted for Susan Collins several times），后跟原因状语从句（because I think...），其中包含宾语从句（that she does care...）。
sentences.analysis: does 表示强调；care about 意为“关心”。

sentences.en: But it's going to be a real problem for me in November.
sentences.cn: 但这在十一月对我来说将是一个真正的问题。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（it）+ 谓语（'s going to be）+ 表语（a real problem）+ 状语（for me in November）。
sentences.analysis: be going to 表示将来；a real problem 意为“一个真正的问题”。

sentences.en: And Max, I'm actually going to start with you, because you're with VoteVets, which means you guys had a dog in the fight in this race.
sentences.cn: 马克斯，我实际上要从你开始，因为你在 VoteVets，这意味着你们在这场竞选中有利害关系。
sentences.structure: 主句（I'm actually going to start with you）+ 原因状语从句（because you're with VoteVets），后跟非限制性定语从句（which means...），其中包含宾语从句（you guys had a dog in the fight）。
sentences.analysis: have a dog in the fight 是习语，意为“有利害关系”。

sentences.en: Democrats' chances of flipping the Maine Senate seat.
sentences.cn: 民主党翻转缅因州参议院席位的机会。
sentences.structure: 名词短语：主语（Democrats' chances）+ 介词短语（of flipping the Maine Senate seat）作定语。
sentences.analysis: flip the seat 意为“翻转席位（改变政党控制）”。

sentences.en: Is this race as close as you all hoped it would be, now that Graham has struggled with these scandals?
sentences.cn: 既然格雷厄姆因这些丑闻而挣扎，这场竞选是否像你们所有人希望的那样接近？
sentences.structure: 一般疑问句（Is this race as close as...）+ 比较结构（as...as...）+ 宾语从句（you all hoped it would be）+ 原因状语从句（now that...）。
sentences.analysis: as close as 表示“和……一样接近”；now that 意为“既然”。

sentences.en: The race is always going to be close.
sentences.cn: 这场竞选总是会很接近。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（The race）+ 谓语（is always going to be）+ 表语（close）。
sentences.analysis: be going to 表示将来；close 在此意为“势均力敌的”。

sentences.en: Not with a five-term senator, it's not.
sentences.cn: 不是面对一位连任五届的参议员，它不是。
sentences.structure: 省略句：完整形式为“It is not close with a five-term senator.”。
sentences.analysis: five-term 是复合形容词；it's not 是省略表达。

sentences.en: What I would say about the Susan Collins race is that we've seen in the past, is you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light or putting forward a Susan Collins light in a race.
sentences.cn: 关于苏珊·柯林斯的竞选，我想说的是，我们在过去看到的是，你不会通过选举一个“苏珊·柯林斯轻量版”或在竞选中推出一个“苏珊·柯林斯轻量版”来赢得这个席位。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What I would say...）+ 系动词（is）+ 表语从句（that we've seen...），其中表语从句又包含另一个表语从句（is you're not going to win...）。
sentences.analysis: Susan Collins light 意为“苏珊·柯林斯的弱化版”，light 表示“轻量级的”；put forward 意为“提出”。

sentences.en: You are going to beat her by energizing your base, particularly in a midterm election.
sentences.cn: 你将通过激励你的基本盘来击败她，尤其是在中期选举中。
sentences.structure: 简单句：主语（You）+ 谓语（are going to beat）+ 宾语（her）+ 方式状语（by energizing your base）+ 插入语（particularly in a midterm election）。
sentences.analysis: energize your base 意为“激励你的基本盘”；midterm election 指“中期选举”。

topics.title: 缅因州参议员选举：普拉特纳 vs. 柯林斯
topics.content: 缅因州民主党初选中，丑闻缠身的格雷厄姆·普拉特纳（Graham Platner）以约75%得票率击败州长珍妮特·米尔斯（Janet Mills），将挑战五届共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯（Susan Collins）。普拉特纳需争取米尔斯支持者，而柯林斯在独立选民中仍有优势。民主党希望翻转该席位，但普拉特纳的品格问题可能影响选情。
topics.keywords: Graham Platner · Susan Collins · primary · flip · independent voter

topics.title: 美国对伊朗军事打击
topics.content: 美国在直升机被击落后对伊朗发动导弹袭击，中东紧张局势升级。此前美伊之间曾有长期承诺的协议，但停火（ceasefire）面临压力。事件可能影响地区稳定及美国外交政策。
topics.keywords: missile · Iran · ceasefire · U.S. military strike · helicopter downed

topics.title: 人工智能公司IPO与泡沫担忧
topics.content: OpenAI和Anthropic计划在年底前进行首次公开募股（IPO），引发市场对人工智能泡沫（A.I. bubble）即将破裂的担忧。投资者关注AI行业估值是否过高，以及IPO是否标志行业转折点。
topics.keywords: IPO · OpenAI · Anthropic · A.I. bubble · pop

topics.title: 科罗拉多州长候选人争议言论
topics.content: 科罗拉多州共和党州长候选人维克多·马克思（Victor Marx）声称自己小时候杀过人，并反问“这重要吗？”，引发舆论哗然。他是共和党初选的领先者（frontrunner），其极端言论可能影响选情。
topics.keywords: Victor Marx · gubernatorial · frontrunner · controversial · killed someone

quiz.question: 在 'Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner' 中，'stick with' 最接近的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'stick with' 是短语动词，意为“继续支持或选择”，这里指民主党人决定继续支持普拉特纳。其他选项为字面义或反义。

quiz.question: 在 'the A.I. bubble is about to pop' 中，'pop' 的含义是？
quiz.explanation: 'pop' 在此处意为“破裂”，指泡沫破裂。经济语境中常用 'bubble pops' 表示泡沫破灭。

quiz.question: 在 'Graham Platner punches his ticket' 中，'punches his ticket' 的意思是？
quiz.explanation: 'punch one's ticket' 是习语，意为“获得提名或资格”，尤其在政治语境中表示成功晋级。

quiz.question: 根据文本，普拉特纳赢得初选后，面临的主要挑战是什么？
options: B. 争取米尔斯支持者
quiz.explanation: 文本提到普拉特纳需要“get Mills voters on his side”，即争取米尔斯支持者的支持，因为米尔斯在初选中失利，其选民可能转向柯林斯。

quiz.question: 关于苏珊·柯林斯，以下哪项描述正确？
options: A. 她与特朗普关系紧密
B. 她是一位五届参议员
quiz.explanation: 文本称柯林斯为“five-term senator”，即五届参议员。她与特朗普关系不紧密（'not a tight bond'），且是共和党人。

quiz.question: 关于人工智能IPO，文本中引用的观点是什么？
quiz.explanation: 文本中引用的观点是“the A.I. bubble is about to pop”，即人工智能泡沫即将破裂，暗示IPO可能是泡沫顶点的信号。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Today in the group chat, Democrats in Maine vote to stick with Graham Platner. Could that cost the party a shot at flipping the Senate this fall? REP. ALEXANDRIA OCASIO-CORTEZ (D-NY): If the choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take health care away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
paragraphs.cn: 今天在群聊中，缅因州的民主党人投票支持格雷厄姆·普拉特纳。这会让他们在今年秋天失去翻转参议院的机会吗？众议员亚历山德里娅·奥卡西奥-科尔特斯（纽约州民主党）：如果选票上的选择是那个和一个投票剥夺数百万美国人医保的参议员，那就是我们必须权衡的情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Missiles light up the sky in the Middle East again. Will the strained ceasefire last long enough for the U.S. to deliver that long- promised deal with Iran? And a big day for the Epstein files. What will Bill Gates tell lawmakers about his links to the late convicted sex offender.
paragraphs.cn: 导弹再次照亮中东的天空。脆弱的停火能否持续足够长的时间，让美国兑现与伊朗的长期承诺协议？爱泼斯坦文件迎来重要一天。比尔·盖茨会就他与已故被定罪性犯罪者的联系向立法者说些什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult? VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: 你认为你成年后杀过人吗？维克托·马克斯（共和党），

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: This is a candidate who also claims he killed someone as a child. Meet the frontrunner in the Republican race for governor of Colorado. And it's the $3.5 trillion question on Wall Street. Has the A.I. boom gotten ahead of itself?
paragraphs.cn: 这是一位自称小时候杀过人的候选人。来认识一下科罗拉多州州长共和党初选的领跑者。这是华尔街的3.5万亿美元问题。人工智能热潮是否已经超前了？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop. GRAHAM PLATNER (D),
paragraphs.cn: OpenAI和Anthropic计划在今年年底前进行首次公开募股，这向我表明人工智能泡沫即将破裂。格雷厄姆·普拉特纳（民主党），

paragraphs.speaker: Maine Senate Candidate
paragraphs.text: To any of those who feel let down or disappointed or disillusioned, it is my job to earn your trust, faith, and support. And I will spend every day of this campaign. And if I have the privilege every day in the United States Senate doing exactly that.
paragraphs.cn: 对于任何感到失望或幻灭的人，我的工作是赢得你们的信任、信念和支持。我会在这场竞选中度过每一天。如果我有幸在美国参议院，每一天都会这样做。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Graham Platner punches his ticket. But now he's got to convince voters to choose him over a five-term senator. Good morning everybody. I'm Audie Cornish. Here is where we begin. That big statement out of Maine. Democrat Graham Platner, despite all the scandals, earned about three- fourths of primary voters' support over Governor Janet Mills. Moments after clinching the nomination, he turned his attention to his new opponent, Republican Senator Susan Collins.
paragraphs.cn: 格雷厄姆·普拉特纳赢得了提名。但现在他必须说服选民选择他而不是一位连任五届的参议员。大家早上好。我是奥迪·科尼什。我们开始吧。来自缅因州的重磅声明。民主党人格雷厄姆·普拉特纳，尽管丑闻缠身，仍赢得了约四分之三初选选民的支持，击败了州长珍妮特·米尔斯。在获得提名后不久，他将注意力转向了他的新对手，共和党参议员苏珊·柯林斯。

paragraphs.speaker: Platner
paragraphs.text: Susan Collins may have started -- may have started her career decades ago in Washington with good intentions, but she has become just as spineless and corrupt as the establishment she now serves. If you are an independent voice, why do you vote with Donald Trump 95 percent of the time?
paragraphs.cn: 苏珊·柯林斯可能几十年前在华盛顿开始她的职业生涯时怀有良好意图，但她已经变得和她现在所服务的建制派一样没有骨气和腐败。如果你是一个独立的声音，为什么95%的时间都投票支持唐纳德·特朗普？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Platner has a lot of work to do to get Mills voters on his side.
paragraphs.cn: 普拉特纳需要做很多工作才能让米尔斯支持者站到他这边。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: I couldn't honestly support Platner, even though some of the things that he says are really good.
paragraphs.cn: 我实在无法支持普拉特纳，尽管他说的有些话确实很好。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: I don't think he's ready to be a senator. I'm leaning towards Collins unless the guy shows something else, but I just -- he's not there yet. And --
paragraphs.cn: 我不认为他已经准备好当参议员了。我倾向于支持柯林斯，除非那家伙表现出其他东西，但我只是 --  -- 他还没到那个程度。而且 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: And so, what will you do in November if its Platner versus Collins?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，如果十一月是普拉特纳对阵柯林斯，你会怎么做？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.text: That's a really good question. I'm a Democrat, but I voted for Susan Collins several times, because I think that she does care about the state of Maine. But it's going to be a real problem for me in November.
paragraphs.cn: 这是个很好的问题。我是民主党人，但我多次投票给苏珊·柯林斯，因为我认为她确实关心缅因州。但十一月对我来说将是一个真正的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, today in the group chat, Mike Leon, host of the "Can We Please Talk" podcast; Jared Stepman, columnist with "The Daily Signal"; and Max Rose, former Democratic congressman from New York. And Max, I'm actually going to start with you, because you're with VoteVets, which means you guys had a dog in the fight in this race. You guys have been giving a lot of money across the country. And what is it? Let me just show what -- what's at stake here. Democrats' chances of flipping the Maine Senate seat.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，今天在群聊中，有《我们能谈谈吗》播客的主持人迈克·莱昂，《每日信号》的专栏作家贾里德·斯特普曼，以及前纽约州民主党众议员马克斯·罗斯。马克斯，我实际上想先问你，因为你在VoteVets工作，这意味着你们在这场竞选中也有利益。你们在全国范围内投入了大量资金。那么情况如何？让我展示一下 --  -- 这里的关键是什么。民主党翻转缅因州参议院席位的机会。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It was pretty good. Maybe this has jeopardized it. We're going to show people a chart while you're talking so you can see what's at stake. Is this race as close as you all hoped it would be, now that Graham has struggled with these scandals? MAX ROSE (D),
paragraphs.cn: 本来相当不错。也许这已经危及到了。我们会在你说话时向人们展示一张图表，这样你就能看到利害关系。既然格雷厄姆在这些丑闻中挣扎，这场竞选是否像你们所有人希望的那样接近？马克斯·罗斯（民主党）：

paragraphs.speaker: Former New York Congressman
paragraphs.text: The race is always going to be close. Because --
paragraphs.cn: 这场竞选总是会很接近。因为 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Not with a five-term senator, it's not. A lot of times it can be a blowout, right? Like what -- how do you look at what he needs to accomplish?
paragraphs.cn: 对于一个连任五届的参议员来说，并非如此。很多时候可能会是一边倒，对吧？比如 --  -- 你怎么看他需要完成什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: Well, I think that we're saying the same thing --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我认为我们说的是同一件事 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: -- in essence, right? Susan Collins is tough to beat. What I would say about the Susan Collins race is that we've seen in the past, is you're not going to win this seat by electing a Susan Collins light or putting forward a Susan Collins light in a race. You are going to beat her by energizing your base, particularly in a midterm election.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 本质上，对吧？苏珊·柯林斯很难被击败。关于苏珊·柯林斯的竞选，我想说的是，我们过去看到的是，你不可能通过选出一个"苏珊·柯林斯轻量版"或是在竞选中推出一个"苏珊·柯林斯轻量版"来赢得这个席位。你要通过激励你的基本盘来击败她，尤其是在中期选举中。

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: There is no doubt, though, that this election has got to be a referendum on Susan Collins: on the ways in which she has voted to empower Donald Trump; on her refusal to hold Donald Trump accountable; on the ways in which she has said, I might be fair, but I want to take healthcare away from millions of people. Says the right thing often; does the wrong thing very frequently.
paragraphs.cn: 不过，毫无疑问，这次选举必须是对苏珊·柯林斯的一次公投：关于她投票赋予唐纳德·特朗普权力的方式；关于她拒绝追究唐纳德·特朗普责任的方式；关于她所说的，我可能公平，但我想从数百万人手中夺走医疗保健的方式。她经常说正确的话；却非常频繁地做错误的事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. So, this is very much the message we hear over and over again. And I love that Platner in front of him, it said, "They don't know Maine." You know what I mean? Usually not his own name, not a bunch of other things. It was like, hello, media, this one is for you. I want to play two clips back-to-back, because it sort of, I think, represents something of this race. First, I'm going to play for you J.D. Vance and how he talks about Susan Collins.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。所以，这正是我们反复听到的信息。我喜欢他面前的普拉特纳的牌子，上面写着"他们不了解缅因州"。你懂我的意思吗？通常不是他自己的名字，也不是其他一堆东西。就像是在说，媒体，这是给你们的。我想连续播放两段音频，因为我认为这代表了这场竞选的一些特点。首先，我要播放J.D.万斯谈论苏珊·柯林斯的片段。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And then I'm going to play AOC and how she's talking about Platner. You tell me how you hear the tone of these. J.D. VANCE (R),
paragraphs.cn: 然后我要播放AOC谈论普拉特纳的片段。你来告诉我你如何感受这些语气。J.D.万斯（共和党）：

paragraphs.speaker: U.S. Vice President
paragraphs.text: Here's the thing I'll say about Susan Collins, is sometimes I get frustrated with Susan Collins. I almost wish that she was more partisan. But the thing I love about Susan is she is independent, because Maine is an independent state. And frankly, if she was as partisan as I sometimes wish that she was, she would not be a good fit for the people of Maine. So, let's give a shout-out to Susan Collins, who's doing a great job.
paragraphs.cn: 关于苏珊·柯林斯，我要说的是，有时我会对她感到沮丧。我几乎希望她更党派化一些。但我喜欢苏珊的一点是，她很独立，因为缅因州是一个独立的州。坦率地说，如果她像我有时希望的那样党派化，她就不适合缅因州的人民。所以，让我们为苏珊·柯林斯喝彩，她做得很好。

paragraphs.speaker: Ocasio-Cortez
paragraphs.text: When it comes to the substance of this reporting, obviously, there's a lot in that behavior that's really challenging. It's hard to stomach, you know, in some of it. But -- but at the end of the day, I think that this is a choice. If that choice on the ballot is between that and a senator who's voted to take healthcare away from millions of Americans, that's the situation that we have to weigh.
paragraphs.cn: 就这份报道的实质内容而言，显然，那种行为中有很多令人非常不安的地方。有些内容让人难以接受。但归根结底，我认为这是一个选择。如果选票上的选择是在那和一位投票剥夺数百万美国人医疗保健的参议员之间，那就是我们必须权衡的情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I played these, because one is like, it's hard to stomach. And the other is like, I get frustrated. Shout-out though, anyway, because I guess Maine. And I was, like, these are not ringing endorsements. MIKE LEON, HOST, "CAN WE PLEASE TALK?"
paragraphs.cn: 我播放这些，是因为一个让人难以接受，另一个让我感到沮丧。不过还是要喝彩，因为我想是缅因州。我当时想，这些都不是热情洋溢的支持。迈克·莱昂，主持人，"我们能谈谈吗？"

paragraphs.speaker: Podcast
paragraphs.text: But Audie, not only are they not ringing endorsements, the voter that we played in the package before listen to her tone. Maine independents are the second largest party in Maine.
paragraphs.cn: 但奥迪，不仅不是热情洋溢的支持，我们在之前的片段中播放的那位选民，听听她的语气。缅因州的独立人士是该州第二大党派。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. And a woman, in particular.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。尤其是女性。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: And a woman and a Democrat who said she voted for Susan Collins, against her will, it sounded like, from that thing. So -- but those are -- those are some of the things that we're going to have to consider now. It's really about the character quality is what something that Graham Platner has been trying to say: Forget about that. I'm from here. It's how she's voted, to Max's point.
paragraphs.cn: 一位女性民主党人，她说她投票给了苏珊·柯林斯，但听起来像是违背了自己的意愿。所以 --  -- 但这些都是我们现在必须考虑的一些事情。这实际上关乎品格特质，这正是格雷厄姆·普拉特纳一直试图说的：忘掉那些。我来自这里。重要的是她的投票记录，正如马克斯所说。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: But then also you hear the voters, and they're, like, a little, still unsure because of those character issues with Platner. So, it's going to be really interesting.
paragraphs.cn: 但你也听到选民们，他们仍然有点不确定，因为普拉特纳的那些品格问题。所以，这将非常有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And he's been telling a story for a while of, like, you know, I was -- went through difficult times. Now I'm a person who has changed. I want to ask one more thing. Collins and Trump, that's not a tight bond. OK? Can I just put it that way? Here's a list I'm going to play for you. Just some moments over the past couple of months where you hear her talking around Trump policies and where she stands on them. SEN. SUSAN COLLINS (R-ME): The 60 days is a trigger.
paragraphs.cn: 而且他一直在讲述一个故事，比如，你知道，我经历过困难时期。现在我是一个改变了的人。我想再问一件事。柯林斯和特朗普，关系并不紧密。好吗？我能这么说吗？这里有一份列表，我要播放给你听。只是过去几个月里的一些时刻，你听到她谈论特朗普的政策以及她对这些政策的立场。参议员苏珊·柯林斯（缅因州共和党）：60天是一个触发点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: After 60 days, in my view, the president has to obtain congressional approval or Congress can block it. I do not believe that individuals who were convicted of violence against police officers on January 6th should be entitled to reimbursement for their legal fees. Concerned about the cuts in Medicaid and the impact on my state, but other states, as well. I've talked with the secretary of homeland security.
paragraphs.cn: 60天后，在我看来，总统必须获得国会批准，否则国会可以阻止。我不认为1月6日被判犯有暴力袭击警察罪的人应该有权获得法律费用报销。我担心医疗补助削减对我州以及其他州的影响。我已经与国土安全部长谈过。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I have strongly recommended to her that there be a pause on the enforcement surge in both Maine and in Minnesota.
paragraphs.cn: 我强烈建议她在缅因州和明尼苏达州暂停执法行动。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, I've chased her in the hallways before, too. She often ends up voting with Republicans, whatever that party line is. But is Trump going to get involved in this simply because of what's at stake with the Senate? Or is he going to send surrogates? He'll be like, OK, J.D., you go there? Like, can she expect any support there? JARRETT STEPMAN, COLUMNIST, "THE DAILY SIGNAL": Yes, I feel that's why he sent J.D. and I feel he will, if he's smart, actually keep some distance from this.
paragraphs.cn: 我以前也在走廊里追过她。她经常最终与共和党人一起投票，无论党派路线如何。但特朗普会仅仅因为参议院的利害关系而介入吗？还是他会派代理人？他会说，好吧，J.D.，你去那里？她能指望得到支持吗？《每日信号》专栏作家贾勒特·斯特普曼：是的，我认为这就是他派J.D.去的原因，而且我认为如果他聪明的话，实际上会与此保持一定距离。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I mean part of the -- the thing at stake here is that they're independent. Maine is a little bit different. I mean, both these candidates come from sort of -- almost independent of the party.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，部分利害关系在于他们是独立的。缅因州有点不同。我的意思是，这两位候选人都来自某种 --  -- 几乎独立于党派。

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: Because they -- they really focus on the issues related to Maine.
paragraphs.cn: 因为他们确实专注于与缅因州相关的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We should say, Platner is recruiting from outside the party. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 我们应该说，普拉特纳正在从党外招募人员。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: Exactly. So, they're both sort of weirdly outsiders, even though Collins has been Republican part of the party for a long time, she's always struck an independent game this entire time. So, I think that's going to continue during this race. That is her path. Because if she does get too close to Trump, that's going to be the argument saying, whoa, she's too MAGA. She's not -- too much for me, which is more or less a blue state.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。所以，他们俩都有点奇怪地是局外人，尽管柯林斯长期以来一直是共和党的一部分，但她一直保持着独立的姿态。所以，我认为这种情况将在这次竞选中继续下去。这是她的道路。因为如果她与特朗普走得太近，就会有人说，哇，她太MAGA了。她不适合我，这基本上是一个蓝州。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, you're saying there's nothing to be gained there of him coming.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，你是说他来没有什么好处。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, you guys stay with me, because we're going to talk about a couple other states this hour, as well. We're also still waiting for news about some sort of proposed deal with Iran. But in the meantime, the U.S. has launched new strikes against Iran overnight. Plus, Epstein's former assistant testifies before Congress. What she says she did not see. And a Texas teenager convicted of killing a rival track star. Why the jury rejected his claim of self-defense.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，你们继续关注，因为本小时我们还要讨论其他几个州。我们还在等待关于与伊朗某种拟议协议的消息。但与此同时，美国在夜间对伊朗发动了新的打击。此外，爱泼斯坦的前助理在国会作证。她说她没有看到什么。还有一名德克萨斯州青少年被判杀害一名竞争对手田径明星。为什么陪审团驳回了他的自卫主张。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: It's just sad that it had to come to this. It was just a bad, bad decision he made.
paragraphs.cn: 事情发展到这一步真是令人难过。他做了一个非常非常糟糕的决定。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Right now, we are following the latest round of escalation between the U.S. and Iran. U.S. CENTRAL COMMAND saying they are now finished hitting targets in Iran in response to an Army helicopter getting shot down over the Strait of Hormuz. Iran's state media, however, released new video overnight, saying they retaliated to the U.S. by attack -- by launching missiles against targets in Bahrain and Kuwait and an air base in Jordan.
paragraphs.cn: 目前，我们正在关注美国与伊朗之间最新一轮的紧张升级。美国中央司令部表示，他们已完成对伊朗境内目标的打击，以回应一架陆军直升机在霍尔木兹海峡被击落的事件。然而，伊朗国家媒体夜间发布了新视频，声称他们通过向巴林、科威特的目标以及约旦的一个空军基地发射导弹，对美国进行了报复。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Joining me now with the latest, CNN international correspondent Paula Hancocks in Abu Dhabi. Paula, can you talk about this? We know Iran, throughout the last couple of weeks, has -- they've tried to spread the war throughout the region. What have you heard from officials in Kuwait or Bahrain about confirmed damage?
paragraphs.cn: 现在连线CNN国际记者宝拉·汉考克斯，她在阿布扎比带来最新消息。宝拉，你能谈谈这个情况吗？我们知道伊朗在过去几周一直试图将战争扩散到整个地区。你从科威特或巴林官员那里听到了关于确认损失的消息吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.text: The next few hours are going to be crucial as to whether or not this is an isolated escalation between the U.S. and Iran, or whether it is a step on the escalatory ladder. Now, at this point, we have not heard any damage assessment or casualty figures from those countries which were targeted by Iran. That is, Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain. Iran said that it had targeted the fifth fleet headquarters in Bahrain.
paragraphs.cn: 接下来的几个小时将至关重要，这决定了这是美国与伊朗之间一次孤立的升级，还是升级阶梯上的一步。目前，我们还没有听到来自伊朗攻击目标国家 --  -- 即约旦、科威特和巴林 --  -- 的任何损失评估或伤亡数字。伊朗表示，他们袭击了巴林的第五舰队总部。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.text: They also say that they targeted a military base in Jordan, Kuwait, as well, saying that it was intercepting incoming aerial missiles and drones. So, what we've heard from Iran's side is that they have said that there will -- no attack will go unanswered from the United States. What we had seen from U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 他们还声称袭击了约旦和科威特的一个军事基地，并表示该基地正在拦截来袭的空中导弹和无人机。我们从伊朗方面听到的是，他们表示任何来自美国的攻击都不会没有回应。我们从美国方面看到的是……

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.text: CENTRAL COMMAND is they said they had carried out what they called a, quote, "proportional response" to the shooting down of that Apache helicopter, saying that there were three locations along the Strait of Hormuz that were targeted. They say that they hit air defense, ground control stations, radar sites, as well. Now, one U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 美国中央司令部表示，他们对那架阿帕奇直升机被击落事件进行了所谓的"对等回应"，称打击了霍尔木兹海峡沿岸的三个地点。他们表示还打击了防空系统、地面控制站和雷达站。现在，一位美国官员告诉CNN，这实际上是为了向伊朗发出警告，他们希望仍能保持谈判的轨道，不会阻碍这些谈判，尽管我们从伊朗外交部发言人那里听到，伊朗正在重新评估谈判，指责美国缺乏谈判诚意，奥迪。

paragraphs.speaker: Paula Hancocks
paragraphs.text: official telling CNN that this was really intended to be a warning to Iran, and they do hope that they will still be able to keep the talks on track, that it won't hinder those talks, although we've heard from Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson that Iran is reviewing negotiations at this point, accusing the U.S. of negotiating in bad faith, Audie.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。这是宝拉·汉考克斯带来的最新消息。非常感谢。现在是整点过17分。我想给你带来早间简报。今天，众议院监督委员会将闭门听取微软亿万富翁比尔·盖茨的证词。他计划就与杰弗里·爱泼斯坦的职业关系作证。盖茨并未被指控有任何不当行为。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. That's Paula Hancocks with that update. Thank you so much. It is now 17 minutes past the hour. I want to give you your morning roundup. Happening today, the House Oversight Committee will hear from Microsoft billionaire Bill Gates behind closed doors. He's scheduled to testify about his professional relationship with Jeffrey Epstein. Gates has not been accused of wrongdoing.
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，爱泼斯坦的前助理莱斯利·格罗夫周二作证称，她"从未目睹任何不当或非法行为"。她还告诉立法者，她曾安排爱泼斯坦与唐纳德·特朗普在特朗普成为总统之前进行多次通话。特朗普总统并未被指控与爱泼斯坦有任何不当行为。在德克萨斯州，一名青少年因刺死一名来自对手队伍的17岁田径运动员而被判谋杀罪，并被判处35年监禁。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Meanwhile, Epstein's former assistant, Lesley Groff, testified on Tuesday, saying that she, quote, "never witnessed anything improper or illegal." She also told lawmakers she arranged multiple calls between Epstein and Donald Trump before he was president. President Trump is not accused of any wrongdoing in connection to Epstein. And in Texas, a teen who fatally stabbed a 17-year-old track athlete from a rival team has been convicted of murder and sentenced to 35 years in prison.
paragraphs.cn: 陪审团驳回了卡梅洛·安东尼关于去年在体育场看台上与奥斯汀·梅特卡夫发生冲突时自卫的说法。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: A jury rejected Karmelo Anthony's claims of self-defense during a confrontation with Austin Metcalf in the stadium bleachers last year.
paragraphs.cn: 事情发展到这一步真是令人难过。他做了一个非常非常糟糕的决定。但他夺走了一条生命。不幸的是，两个年轻的生命因此受到了如此负面的影响。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: It's just sad that it had to come to this. It was just a bad, bad decision he made. But he took a life. And it's unfortunate that two young lives are affected so negatively about this.
paragraphs.cn: 真遗憾事情发展到这一步。他做了一个非常糟糕的决定。但他夺走了一条生命。不幸的是，两个年轻的生命因此受到了如此负面的影响。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Anthony did not testify. His mother took the stand during the sentencing phase, telling jurors her son was sorry. And CNN now projecting Republican Steve Hilton will advance to the November election in the California governor's race against Democrat Xavier Becerra. Becerra, the former health secretary, finished on top with nearly 28 percent of the vote, three points ahead of Hilton, a former FOX News host who has President Trump's endorsement.
paragraphs.cn: 安东尼没有作证。他的母亲在量刑阶段出庭，告诉陪审员她的儿子很抱歉。CNN现在预测共和党人史蒂夫·希尔顿将晋级11月加州州长选举，对阵民主党人泽维尔·贝塞拉。前卫生部长贝塞拉以近28%的得票率位居榜首，领先前福克斯新闻主持人、获得特朗普总统支持的希尔顿三个百分点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: STEVE HILTON (R), CALIFORNIA GUBERNATORIAL CANDIDATE; Californians will have a real choice for change in November. Whether they want to keep going with the policies that have brought us to the highest poverty rate, the highest unemployment rate, the highest cost of living in the country, where they want more of that with Xavier Becerra, or whether they want to go in a new direction.
paragraphs.cn: 史蒂夫·希尔顿（共和党），加州州长候选人：加州人在11月将有一个真正的变革选择。他们是希望继续推行那些导致我们国家最高贫困率、最高失业率、最高生活成本的政策，还是希望与泽维尔·贝塞拉一起继续这样，或者希望走向新的方向。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I have to ask quickly: if Hilton does well, is California still going to be the corrupt election state that Republicans are saying it is?
paragraphs.cn: 我必须快速问一下：如果希尔顿表现良好，加州还会是共和党人所说的腐败选举州吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: Yes. I mean, the question is how well, of course. How much can he actually make some --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我的意思是，问题当然是他表现有多好。他实际上能取得多少 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Is there a certain threshold after which --
paragraphs.cn: 是否存在某个门槛，超过之后 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: -- it's no longer considered corrupt if a Republican wins?
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 如果共和党人获胜，就不再被视为腐败？

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: Well, you know, you know what? Especially given the state, it'll depend on how the votes are counted. I feel that especially after the next --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你知道吗？特别是考虑到这个州，这将取决于选票如何计算。我觉得尤其是在下一次 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. But you can see why I'm asking, right? Like, if he wins, it's fine. But if he doesn't win, it's corrupt.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但你能明白我为什么这么问，对吧？比如，如果他赢了，那就没问题。但如果他没赢，那就是腐败。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But if he wins, it's still corrupt, but somehow --
paragraphs.cn: 但如果他赢了，仍然是腐败的，但不知何故 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: Maybe, maybe. I think it's still fairly unlikely that he's going to win this race. I will also say that it's been the process in California taking so long to discover who --
paragraphs.cn: 也许吧，也许。我认为他赢得这场竞选的可能性仍然很小。我还要说，加州确定人选的过程耗时太长 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, everyone's talking about that.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，每个人都在谈论这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: -- is going to win it. Everybody's talking about this.
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 将会赢得它。每个人都在谈论这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But everyone's disagreeing on what that means.
paragraphs.cn: 但每个人对此的含义意见不一。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That's by definition nefarious.
paragraphs.cn: 这本身就是邪恶的。

paragraphs.speaker: Rose
paragraphs.text: But it makes people uncomfortable with the results.
paragraphs.cn: 但这让人们对结果感到不安。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I have to mention one other thing, just because the number's astounding. Tom Steyer's spending: 215 million. I'm not good at long division, so I didn't want to know how much per voter that spend was, and I can't do it. I can't do it. And someone on -- online was saying, oh, have you ever heard of effective altruism, and that this was ineffective altruism, basically.
paragraphs.cn: 我还得提另一件事，只是因为数字惊人。汤姆·斯泰尔的支出：2.15亿美元。我不擅长长除法，所以不想知道这相当于每个选民花了多少钱，我也算不出来。我算不出来。网上有人说，哦，你听说过有效利他主义吗？而这基本上就是无效利他主义。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: Basically, an ROI in the business term, right? You need a return on that investment. This is twice for Steyer now. He has not gotten that ROI. When is enough, enough? If I had that kind of money, I would invest it in something else. He has tried this political acumen.
paragraphs.cn: 基本上，用商业术语来说就是投资回报率，对吧？你需要投资回报。这对斯泰尔来说是第二次了。他没有得到那个投资回报率。什么时候才够？如果我有那么多钱，我会投资到别的地方。他已经尝试了这种政治手腕。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: It has not worked out.
paragraphs.cn: 但并没有成功。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. I was about to say --
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我正要说的 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: Voters have said that.
paragraphs.cn: 选民们已经这么说了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The voters have rejected it.
paragraphs.cn: 选民们已经否决了它。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: Especially with the billionaire. When the billionaire class. We've got AOC and others that have talked about the billionaire class, and we have somebody here spending his own money like this, it seems like a waste where it could be invested in other.
paragraphs.cn: 尤其是那位亿万富翁。当谈到亿万富翁阶层时，我们有AOC和其他人谈论过亿万富翁阶层，而现在有人像这样花自己的钱，这似乎是一种浪费，这些钱本可以投资到其他地方。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, it's been a wild race coming from last year to all of a sudden have Becerra at the front.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，从去年开始，这场竞选一直很激烈，突然间贝塞拉就领先了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: We couldn't have seen that then.
paragraphs.cn: 我们当时可没预料到这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, after the break on CNN THIS MORNING, two controversial to win? So, the Colorado frontrunner for governor claims he killed a man when he was 7 years old. I have more stories like that from him. Plus, A.I. companies are rushing to the stock market. Is it the next tech boom or is the A.I. bubble about to burst? And for viewers watching us from right here in New York City, Knicks versus Spurs again tonight at the Garden. Good morning.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，在CNN《今晨》节目休息之后，两个有争议的胜利？科罗拉多州州长竞选领跑者声称他7岁时杀了一个人。我还有很多关于他的类似故事。另外，人工智能公司正争相上市。这是下一个科技繁荣，还是人工智能泡沫即将破裂？对于在纽约市观看我们节目的观众，今晚尼克斯队将在花园球馆再次对阵马刺队。早上好。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, a bunch of IPOs in the A.I. sector are about to hit Wall Street after OpenAI announced on Monday it filed for an initial public offering, an IPO. The three biggest A.I. companies are now in line to go public, as well. That includes Anthropic and SpaceX. A fourth, Perplexity, is expected to file in 2028.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，在OpenAI周一宣布提交首次公开募股（IPO）申请后，一批人工智能领域的IPO即将登陆华尔街。三家最大的人工智能公司也即将上市，包括Anthropic和SpaceX。第四家公司Perplexity预计将在2028年提交申请。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.text: We're talking about it. Where the American people can benefit from the success of A.I. And by doing that, they're going to like it better. Because we're leading China. We're leading everybody in the world with A.I., and we want to keep it that way. It's like you make them a partnership in this revolution. It would be a beautiful thing.
paragraphs.cn: 我们正在讨论这个问题。美国人民可以从人工智能的成功中受益。通过这样做，他们会更喜欢它。因为我们在人工智能领域领先中国，领先世界上所有国家，我们希望保持这种状态。这就像让他们成为这场革命的合作伙伴。这将是一件美好的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, investors who get in early could make a fortune. But has the A.I. boom gotten ahead of itself? Madison Mills joins the group chat. She's the senior A.I. reporter at Axios. You guys have done some fantastic reporting on this industry. Just to show people the amount of money at stake. When you look at OpenAI and SpaceX and Anthropic, there were 3.5 trillion. We dug around, and we found out that's, like, as much money as, you know, France. Like, OK? So, we're dealing with a lot of money.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，早期入场的投资者可能会赚大钱。但人工智能的繁荣是否已经超前了？麦迪逊·米尔斯加入了群聊。她是Axios的高级人工智能记者。你们对这个行业做了一些精彩的报道。只是为了向人们展示涉及的资金规模。当你看到OpenAI、SpaceX和Anthropic时，总价值达3.5万亿美元。我们挖掘了一下，发现这相当于法国的经济规模，明白吗？所以我们面对的是巨额资金。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But is it a bubble? And I ask because we've got some people on TikTok who think that it is. I'm going to play for you, the finance experts online.
paragraphs.cn: 但这是泡沫吗？我问这个问题是因为TikTok上有些人认为这是泡沫。我来给你们播放一下网上的金融专家的看法。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: A.I. companies are now trying to save themselves by applying for IPOs.
paragraphs.cn: 人工智能公司现在正试图通过申请IPO来拯救自己。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: OpenAI and Anthropic plan to have IPOs at release before the end of this year, signaling to me that the A.I. bubble is about to pop and that these insiders and institutional investors know that they better cash out now, or they may not ever get the chance.
paragraphs.cn: OpenAI和Anthropic计划在今年年底前进行IPO，这向我表明人工智能泡沫即将破裂，这些内部人士和机构投资者知道他们最好现在套现，否则可能再也没有机会了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Why is this the word on the street?
paragraphs.cn: 为什么这是坊间传闻？

paragraphs.speaker: Madison Mills
paragraphs.text: Yes, I would push back on a lot of the financial finfluencing that we hear about on finance.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我会反驳很多我们在金融领域听到的金融网红言论。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Finfluencing. You said it. Go on.
paragraphs.cn: 金融网红。你说得对。继续。

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: Yes. But -- but just because companies are IPOing doesn't necessarily mean that the bubble is going to happen this Friday when SpaceX lists on the
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但是 --  -- 仅仅因为公司上市并不一定意味着泡沫会在本周五SpaceX上市时发生。

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: Right? But the concept of the bubble is this idea that valuations are completely divorced from reality: the underlying business fundamentals, the things that we would learn about in journalism and business school --
paragraphs.cn: 对吧？但泡沫的概念是估值完全脱离现实：脱离我们在新闻和商学院学到的 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: -- that make a company profitable, right?
paragraphs.cn: --  -- 那些让公司盈利的基本面，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, the street phrase would be they're high on their own supply?
paragraphs.cn: 所以，行话就是他们自产自销？

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: Absolutely. And that's very clear with a company like SpaceX. Their expected valuation is $1.8 trillion. They're trading at about 90 times their sales -- not their profits, just their sales -- as of this Friday, when they're expected to list. So, that is an example of a company you might look at and say, not sure if the valuation is high.
paragraphs.cn: 完全正确。像SpaceX这样的公司就很明显。他们的预期估值为1.8万亿美元。截至本周五他们预计上市时，其交易价格约为销售额的90倍 --  -- 不是利润，只是销售额。所以，这是一个例子，你可能会看着它说，不确定估值是否过高。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I know. There's also this thing I've been learning about circular financing.
paragraphs.cn: 我知道。我还了解到一种叫循环融资的东西。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, I'm understanding this as, you use money from one A.I. company to do work in your A.I. company. and then another A.I. company gets work from you. It's like the same money going around and around. Do I have that right?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，我的理解是，你从一家人工智能公司拿钱，用于你的人工智能公司的工作，然后另一家人工智能公司又从你这里获得工作。这就像同一笔钱在转来转去。我理解得对吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: Absolutely. That's perfect. And part of --
paragraphs.cn: 完全正确。说得很好。而且部分 --  --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I'm ready to finfluence.
paragraphs.cn: 我准备好搞金融影响力了。

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: You're ready to finfluence. And more than that, you can be the CFO of an A.I. company. You know? But part of the problem with that is that Anthropic can -- or OpenAI, any of the A.I. labs, can book that as revenue. And that feeds into that valuation number that we're getting. But that revenue could also just be money that they are getting from Google, that then they're giving back to Google in the form of compute capacity. And then Google has that compute capacity and is giving it over to another A.I. lab.
paragraphs.cn: 你准备好搞金融影响力了。不仅如此，你还可以成为一家人工智能公司的首席财务官，你知道吗？但问题的一部分在于，Anthropic 或 OpenAI，任何人工智能实验室，都可以把这笔钱记为收入。而这又会影响我们看到的估值数字。但那些收入也可能只是他们从谷歌那里拿到的钱，然后他们又以算力的形式返还给谷歌。然后谷歌拥有那些算力，再把它交给另一个人工智能实验室。

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: And so, it's all very circular right now. And that's the concern.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，现在这一切都非常循环。这就是令人担忧的地方。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And it's giving shell game. I'm going to listen to you, because I believe that this market is the future. I understand that, but I think people rightly have questions. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 这就像在玩把戏。我会听你的，因为我相信这个市场是未来。我明白这一点，但我认为人们有疑问是合理的。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: I'm going to listen to you because I believe that this market is the future. I understand that. But I think people, rightly, have questions. There are a ton of people who are pushing back on A.I. right now. You've got some local municipalities super upset about data centers coming near them. Yesterday we played Brad Paisley in Nashville, upset there might be a data center next to the zoo.
paragraphs.cn: 我会听你的，因为我相信这个市场是未来。我明白这一点。但我认为人们有疑问是合理的。现在有很多人在抵制人工智能。一些地方政府对数据中心建在它们附近非常不满。昨天我们在纳什维尔播放了布拉德·佩斯利的节目，那里的人对动物园旁边可能建一个数据中心感到不满。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: Here at CNN I want to disclose, we're currently suing Perplexity, alleging that the company is unlawfully copying and sharing CNN content in its summaries. I've also heard of people complaining about the summaries as well. How does that figure in to how investors look at this industry, which is facing massive blowback? MADISON MILLS, SENIOR A.I. REPORTER, "AXIOS": Yes, this A.I. hate wave that we're seeing could be a real problem for these A.I.
paragraphs.cn: 在CNN，我想披露一下，我们目前正在起诉Perplexity，指控该公司在其摘要中非法复制和分享CNN的内容。我也听到有人抱怨这些摘要。这如何影响投资者看待这个面临巨大反弹的行业？《Axios》资深人工智能记者麦迪逊·米尔斯：是的，我们看到的这股人工智能仇恨浪潮对这些人工智能公司来说可能是一个真正的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.text: companies because they need something called compute that's like the currency, the energy fueling the A.I. race. If the A.I. labs can't get their data centers, then they can't get the compute that they need to service your queries.
paragraphs.cn: 因为它们需要一种叫做算力的东西，这就像货币，是推动人工智能竞赛的能源。如果人工智能实验室无法获得数据中心，那么它们就无法获得处理用户查询所需的算力。

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: So, it could be a big problem. And we're seeing this data center pushback really hitting a fever pitch.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，这可能是一个大问题。我们看到这种对数据中心的抵制真的达到了白热化程度。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, because some of them are going -- are supposed to be coming up with their own way to power -- to sort of self-power the data centers.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，因为其中一些数据中心将 --  -- 应该会想出自己供电的方式 --  -- 某种程度上实现自我供电。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But, like, people don't want them. And I was thinking, when I look at how people talk about A.I., for instance, we played the president earlier and he said, "we're talking about it where the American people can benefit from the success of A.I. They're going to like it better because we're leading China, we're leading everyone in the world. We want to keep it that way." And I felt like, if you took away the word A.I. and put "globalization," we've heard this before.
paragraphs.cn: 但是，人们并不想要它们。我在想，当我看到人们如何谈论人工智能时，比如我们之前播放了总统的讲话，他说："我们正在讨论如何让美国人民从人工智能的成功中受益。他们会更喜欢它，因为我们领先于中国，领先于世界上所有国家。我们希望保持这种状态。"我觉得，如果把"人工智能"这个词换成"全球化"，我们以前就听过这种说法。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And I wonder if that is contributing to what you call the hate wave.
paragraphs.cn: 我想知道这是否导致了您所说的"仇恨浪潮"。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: People feeling like, I'm being told something's going to make my life better. And my experience in the past is that that isn't -- doesn't play out the way politicians say it will.
paragraphs.cn: 人们觉得，有人告诉我某件事会让我的生活变得更好，而我过去的经验是，这并不会像政客们说的那样实现。

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: I mean, I would push back on that too because the overwhelming research shows that people are not happy about A.I. There's this YouGov poll people cite all the time that showed very clear bipartisan support against A.I. across the aisle.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，我也会反驳这一点，因为绝大多数研究表明，人们对人工智能并不满意。人们经常引用的YouGov民调显示，两党对人工智能的反对意见非常明确，跨越了党派界限。

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: And we're seeing that play out with the data center pushback that you had mentioned. I also do think part of the challenge is that these models are getting better so much faster than anything we've seen.
paragraphs.cn: 我们看到，您提到的数据中心遭到抵制的情况正在上演。我也确实认为，部分挑战在于这些模型改进的速度比我们以往看到的任何事物都要快得多。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. Anthropic releasing a safer work mythos.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。Anthropic发布了一个更安全的工作神话。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Which is still scary.
paragraphs.cn: 这仍然令人恐惧。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: After saying that they were so worried about it they only brought it to the government and certain companies.
paragraphs.cn: 在表示他们对此非常担忧之后，他们只将其提供给了政府和某些公司。

paragraphs.speaker: Mills
paragraphs.text: Exactly. And because of that, I think that people haven't necessarily seen a lot of the benefits of A.I. because it's getting better so much faster. And so people are, you know, hearing about a data center in their backyard, they're hearing A.I. CEOs say that it could replace their jobs. And, by the way, you haven't really felt the positive use cases of it yet because it's getting more technical more quickly. So, I think that creates kind of a challenging soup for people. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 没错。正因为如此，我认为人们不一定看到了人工智能的很多好处，因为它改进得太快了。所以，人们听到自家后院要建数据中心，听到人工智能CEO说它可能取代他们的工作。而且，顺便说一句，你还没有真正感受到它的积极用例，因为它技术提升得太快了。所以，我认为这给人们制造了一种棘手的局面。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. We'll see what happens when people are ringing that bell on Wall Street. Madison Mills, thank you so much for being here. Always love to have you. And straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, the World Cup kicks off tomorrow. But with sky high ticket prices, problems with immigration, is this going to be the World Cup of chaos? Plus, Nancy Mace broke with President Trump over the Epstein files. Is that what costs her a shot at the governor's mansion in South Carolina, or was it something else?
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我们拭目以待华尔街敲钟时会发生什么。麦迪逊·米尔斯，非常感谢你的到来。我们总是很高兴你来。接下来在CNN《今晨》节目中，世界杯明天开赛。但门票价格高昂，移民问题频出，这届世界杯会是一场混乱吗？另外，南希·梅斯因爱泼斯坦文件与特朗普总统决裂。这是否让她失去了南卡罗来纳州州长职位的机会，还是另有原因？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, the World Cup kicks off tomorrow. I know, I said it. It kicks off across North America. But are the off the field headlines threatening to overshadow the biggest tournament in the world? So, there was a Somali referee turned away by U.S. Customs and Border Patrol in Miami when he arrived for his World Cup assignment. And he received a hero's welcome when he returned to Somalia. The Trump administration tells CNN that vetting uncovered derogatory information. They did not go into further detail.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，世界杯明天开赛。我知道，我说了。它将在北美各地开赛。但场外新闻是否会威胁到这项全球最大赛事的关注度？一名索马里裁判在迈阿密被美国海关和边境保护局拒绝入境，他原本是来执行世界杯任务的。他返回索马里时受到了英雄般的欢迎。特朗普政府告诉CNN，审查发现了不利信息，但没有透露更多细节。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, the ref told "The New York Times," quote, "I'm just simply a referee who's trying to live his dream." Fans from Scotland said their permits were withdrawn at the final moment. Teams from Senegal, Uzbekistan were treated, quote, "like criminals," according to critics. And a player from Iraq was questioned for seven hours upon arrival in Chicago.
paragraphs.cn: 这位裁判告诉《纽约时报》："我只是一个试图实现梦想的裁判。"来自苏格兰的球迷表示，他们的许可证在最后一刻被撤销。批评者称，塞内加尔和乌兹别克斯坦的球队受到了"像罪犯一样"的对待。一名伊拉克球员在抵达芝加哥后被审问了七个小时。

paragraphs.speaker: Ian Wright
paragraphs.text: Every few hours it's another story. Another story about fans denied, players denied, officials denied, journalists denied. Now refs. You know something? I'm laughing, but it's not funny. It's actually not funny. Something has to be said. It's expensive tickets. Most expensive tickets ever. Know what? This is the World Cup. This is a World Cup of chaos.
paragraphs.cn: 每隔几个小时就有新故事。又一个关于球迷被拒、球员被拒、官员被拒、记者被拒的故事。现在轮到裁判了。你知道吗？我在笑，但这并不好笑。实际上一点也不好笑。必须说点什么。门票昂贵，史上最贵。你知道吗？这是世界杯。这是一届混乱的世界杯。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Joining us in the group chat, Dan Courtemanche. He's the executive vice president and chief communications officer for Major League Soccer. Did I say your name right? I don't want your mom being mad at me.
paragraphs.cn: 加入我们群聊的是丹·库尔特曼什，他是美国职业足球大联盟的执行副总裁兼首席通讯官。我说对你的名字了吗？我不想让你妈妈生我的气。

paragraphs.speaker: Dan Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: You did. Thank you. It's terrific to be here. Really appreciate it.
paragraphs.cn: 你说对了。谢谢。很高兴来到这里。非常感谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: So, I wanted to talk to you about this for a couple of reasons. One, your team sent over some information here, and I was noting that there is a record number of MLS players, 45, who represent countries in this, right?
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我想和你谈谈这个，有几个原因。第一，你的团队发来了一些信息，我注意到有创纪录的45名MLS球员代表各自国家参赛，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And that you've also got -- done a lot of work through your academies to be a place to recruit people who will want to play here. Does this -- these headlines, do they kind of undermine that message that MLS has been so good, frankly, at cultivating over the last decade?
paragraphs.cn: 而且你们还通过青训体系做了大量工作，成为招募那些想在这里踢球的人的地方。这些新闻是否削弱了MLS在过去十年中精心培养的形象？

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: Yes, I think what we're going to see is some of those headlines tomorrow when the World Cup opens in Mexico City, then certainly in North America and Canada and the United States on Friday. It's going to shift from some of those headlines, the World Cup of chaos, to celebration, to pageantry, to a party.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我认为明天世界杯在墨西哥城开幕时，我们将会看到一些这样的新闻，当然周五在北美、加拿大和美国也会如此。焦点将从那些"混乱世界杯"的新闻转向庆祝、盛况和派对。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: But I'm saying, for the players, is this irritating, scary, frustrating? The whole point is international cooperation.
paragraphs.cn: 但我想说，对于球员来说，这是令人恼火、害怕、沮丧的吗？整个意义在于国际合作。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: It's not feeling welcoming.
paragraphs.cn: 这让人感觉不太受欢迎。

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: So, I hear you. You know, ultimately, that's a question for the players. It's not something that we've seen. And in Major League Soccer, we have players from 78 different countries. And we've been around for 31 seasons. And we haven't experienced that, challenges with visa issues or anything. But I understand where you're coming from. We want to be welcoming to all the players. We want to see them have a wonderful time here in the United States, Canada and Mexico.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我听到了你的意见。你知道，归根结底，这是球员们的问题。我们并没有看到这种情况。在美国职业足球大联盟，我们有来自78个不同国家的球员。我们已经运营了31个赛季。我们没有遇到过签证问题之类的挑战。但我理解你的想法。我们希望欢迎所有球员。我们希望看到他们在美国、加拿大和墨西哥度过愉快的时光。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And a lot of your incoming star players, over the last couple of years, coming to the U.S., they're coming from abroad. It's finally a place people are starting to come to and want to play, right?
paragraphs.cn: 过去几年，很多即将加盟的明星球员来到美国，他们来自国外。这里终于成为了人们开始向往并愿意来踢球的地方，对吧？

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: Most definitely. The greatest of all time. Lionel Messi, by the way, scored last night in Auburn, Alabama, in a friendly against Iceland. You'll see him on Tuesday in Kansas City against Algeria. I hope he scores a lot of goals because then you can watch him playing for Inter Miami each and every weekend in Major League Soccer.
paragraphs.cn: 毫无疑问。史上最伟大的球员。顺便说一句，莱昂内尔·梅西昨晚在阿拉巴马州奥本对阵冰岛的热身赛中进球了。你将在周二堪萨斯城对阵阿尔及利亚的比赛中看到他。我希望他能进很多球，这样你就可以在美国职业足球大联盟每个周末观看他为国际迈阿密队效力。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Now, what I loved about that person who spoke at the end of our introduction is he goes, oh, I laugh because, you know, it's sort of -- sort of sad. And then he tied all of that to ticket prices. And I thought that was really fascinating, that people are also looking at this in the context of the cost of the ticket. The average get in price for a World Cup match right now, roughly $600 for the opening round of the tournament. And people are complaining about FIFA's pricing scheme.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，我喜欢我们介绍结束时发言的那个人，他说，哦，我笑了，因为你知道，这有点 --  -- 有点可悲。然后他把这一切和票价联系起来。我觉得这很有趣，人们也在从票价的角度看待这个问题。目前世界杯比赛的平均入场价格，小组赛阶段大约600美元。人们正在抱怨国际足联的定价方案。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And right now, if I want, I can still get a ticket to USA Paraguay. It's not sold out, and it should be.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，如果我想，我仍然可以买到美国对巴拉圭的门票。比赛还没有售罄，但本应售罄。

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: Yes, I can tell you, I'm going to be there for the U.S.- Paraguay game. I certainly anticipate it's going to be sold out. I got to tell you, there's unprecedented demand for the World Cup.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我可以告诉你，我会去观看美国对巴拉圭的比赛。我预计这场比赛肯定会售罄。我得告诉你，世界杯的需求是前所未有的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Then why isn't it sold out?
paragraphs.cn: 那为什么还没售罄？

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: Much like you'll see from my Knicks tonight, where the get-in price is about $15,000 for lower level seating. Now, that said, I think you're going to see the pageantry, the celebration throughout the World Cup. But I understand where people are coming from. Also, you have to remind people that FIFA is actually a non-profit. So, all that money goes back to the 211 FIFA nations to build the sport, especially in some underdeveloped countries.
paragraphs.cn: 就像你今晚会看到我的尼克斯队比赛一样，下层座位的入场价格大约是15000美元。话虽如此，我认为你会看到世界杯期间的盛况和庆祝活动。但我理解人们的想法。另外，你必须提醒人们，国际足联实际上是一个非营利组织。所以，所有的钱都会返还给211个国际足联成员国，用于发展足球运动，尤其是在一些欠发达国家。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, I'm glad you brought that up because we did have a California lawmaker, Representative Sydney Kamlager-Dove. She was on the show Friday. She said something I didn't expect to hear related to ticket prices and what her concerns were. Here's what she had to say. REP. SYDNEY KAMLAGER-DOVE (D-CA): The issue is dynamic pricing. The other issue is, you buy a ticket, you don't know where that seat is going to be. You're spending over $1,000.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，很高兴你提到这一点，因为我们确实有一位加州议员，众议员西德尼·卡姆拉格-多夫。她周五参加了节目。她说了一些我没想到会听到的话，关于票价和她的担忧。以下是她的发言。众议员西德尼·卡姆拉格-多夫（加州民主党）：问题在于动态定价。另一个问题是，你买了票，却不知道座位在哪里。你要花超过1000美元。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: And finally you got the -- you get the seat and you're behind a post and you can't see anything. We want to know why ticket prices are changing so dramatically from city to city. I want to know why cities aren't getting any financial support from the World Cup to actually put on the games, because World Cup is getting everything, concessions, merchandise, parking, tickets.
paragraphs.cn: 最后你拿到了座位，却坐在柱子后面，什么也看不见。我们想知道为什么票价在不同城市之间变化如此之大。我想知道为什么城市没有从世界杯获得任何财政支持来举办比赛，因为世界杯拿走了所有东西：特许经营权、商品、停车位、门票。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: That was just a long list. Can you answer any of it?
paragraphs.cn: 这问题可真不少。你能回答其中任何一个吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: Well, I can tell you, look, we're fortunate, five of the stadiums out of the 13 markets in the United States and Canada are Major League Soccer stadiums. And I can tell you that nobody will be sitting behind posts during the World Cup games in those MLS stadiums. So, look, she brings up some valid points. She's clearly an expert on it. But we think it's going to pivot again from some of the challenges that we've seen in the media to the celebration.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我可以告诉你，我们很幸运，美国和加拿大13个市场中有5个体育场是美国职业足球大联盟的球场。我可以告诉你，在世界杯比赛期间，那些MLS球场里没有人会坐在柱子后面。她提出了一些合理的观点，她显然是这方面的专家。但我们认为，焦点将从媒体看到的一些挑战转向庆祝活动。

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: And ultimately goals scored by hopefully the United States national team and they make a deep run in the tournament.
paragraphs.cn: 最终，希望美国国家队能进球，并在赛事中走得更远。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes. I think, fundamentally, nobody is arguing that it won't be good. They'll be arguing about whether or not they got to participate.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我认为，基本上没有人会质疑这将是好事。他们会争论的是他们是否能够参与其中。

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: Yes. You know, and I -- you know, it's also a good reminder that FIFA actually had $60 tickets available for those passionate supporters who registered early. So, they had some affordable prices. But I certainly understand you. And for us, you also have to look at, there'll be nearly 80 fan festivals throughout the different markets. Twenty of our MLS clubs are going to have soccer celebrations. So, you're providing free accessibility to fans who may not be able to go to the games.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。你知道，这也提醒我们，国际足联实际上为那些提前注册的热情球迷提供了60美元的门票。所以，他们有一些可负担的价格。但我当然理解你。对我们来说，你也要看到，不同市场将有近80个球迷节。我们的20个MLS俱乐部将举办足球庆祝活动。这样，那些可能无法去现场看球的球迷也能免费参与。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Well, thank you, Dan, so much for being here. Good luck. I know this is your Super Bowl. That's what we say here in the U.S. instead of soccer.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。非常感谢你来到这里，丹。祝你好运。我知道这是你的超级碗 --  -- 我们在美国是这么说的，而不是足球。

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: Hey, I'm a big -- I'm a University of Georgia graduate. I love gridiron football. Come on.
paragraphs.cn: 嘿，我可是个铁杆 --  -- 我是佐治亚大学的毕业生。我热爱美式橄榄球。真的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Oh, OK. OK. All right. Well, thank you for being here. Appreciate your time.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，好的。好的。好吧。感谢你来到这里。感谢你的时间。

paragraphs.speaker: Courtemanche
paragraphs.text: Really appreciate it.
paragraphs.cn: 非常感谢。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Next on CNN THIS MORNING, Congresswoman Nancy Mace gets crushed in the South Carolina governor's race. Did going against Trump cost her the election? But first.
paragraphs.cn: 接下来在CNN《今晨》节目中，国会女议员南希·梅斯在南卡罗来纳州州长竞选中惨败。与特朗普对抗是否让她输掉了选举？但首先。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.text: If you're elected governor, would you continue to perform exorcisms while governor?
paragraphs.cn: 如果你当选州长，你会继续在任期内进行驱魔吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Yes, controversial candidate leading in the polls in Colorado. Will the strange headlines sway voters?
paragraphs.cn: 是的，科罗拉多州有争议的候选人在民调中领先。这些奇怪的标题会影响选民吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Kyle Clark
paragraphs.text: Your campaign website falsely claimed that you had rescued more than 45,000 women and children. You once called in a U.S. military airstrike that killed 70 ISIS fighters. You perform exorcisms, commanding demons to come out of people. Do you understand why so many of your critics think you're a con man? VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: 你的竞选网站虚假声称你拯救了超过45000名妇女和儿童。你曾呼叫美军空袭，炸死了70名ISIS武装分子。你进行驱魔，命令恶魔从人们身上出来。你明白为什么这么多批评者认为你是个骗子吗？维克多·马克斯（共和党人），

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK, that was a wild ride. And it didn't stop there. That reporter in Colorado was interviewing a Republican frontrunner for governor, Victor Marx. He's a Marine Corps veteran. He runs a faith- based nonprofit. But he's made a lot of outlandish claims. And it does not end with exorcisms and rescuing women and children from slavery. He also says he killed a man when he was a child.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧，那真是一段疯狂的对话。而且还没完。科罗拉多州的那位记者正在采访共和党州长竞选领跑者维克多·马克斯。他是一名海军陆战队老兵。他经营着一个信仰非营利组织。但他发表了许多离谱的言论。而且不仅仅是驱魔和从奴役中拯救妇女儿童。他还说他小时候杀过人。

paragraphs.speaker: Kyle Clark
paragraphs.text: Your claim that your abusive stepfather forced you to kill a man when you were seven years old, is that the only person you've ever killed? VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: 你声称你虐待你的继父强迫你在七岁时杀了一个人，那是你唯一杀过的人吗？维克多·马克斯（共和党人），

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Candidate For Governor
paragraphs.text: Well, I would say, as a -- as a child, yes. But I've been in other situations where, you know, possibly people or persons died.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我想说，作为 --  -- 作为一个孩子，是的。但我也经历过其他情况，你知道，可能有人或一些人死亡。

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.text: Do you think that you've killed people as an adult?
paragraphs.cn: 你认为你成年后杀过人吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.text: Killing somebody is a pretty weighty thing. So, I'm just asking you, how many people have you killed?
paragraphs.cn: 杀人是一件相当沉重的事情。所以，我只是问你，你杀了多少人？

paragraphs.speaker: Marx
paragraphs.text: Well, if I did, I wouldn't be telling a reporter sitting here in my training center.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，如果我杀了，我不会告诉坐在我训练中心的记者。

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.text: How about voters? Would you tell voters?
paragraphs.cn: 那选民呢？你会告诉选民吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Marx
paragraphs.text: Theres no need. I don't think that's important.
paragraphs.cn: 没必要。我觉得那不重要。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: OK. Group chat is back. I wanted to talk about this because we started the hour talking about Maine, right, and Graham Platner and being like, voters, what are they thinking? You know, like, what's happening? What will these scandals matter? And then I was seeing a lot more stories about what's happening in Colorado. Just to give you a sense of where things are. In this Republican primary right now, the fundraising, Victor Marx is at $2.7 million.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。群聊回来了。我想谈谈这个，因为我们这小时一开始就在讨论缅因州，对吧，还有格雷厄姆·普拉特纳，选民们在想什么？你知道，发生了什么？这些丑闻会有什么影响？然后我看到更多关于科罗拉多州发生的事情的报道。让你了解一下情况。在这次共和党初选中，目前筹款方面，维克托·马克思有270万美元。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: The next closest person is at around $500,000, who is a state senator. I know you're just kind of clocking into this, Jarrett, but help me understand why he's so far ahead in the money. That means people are the donors. The people with pockets are giving to him. JARRETT STEPMAN, CONTRIBUTOR, "THE DAILY SIGNAL": Yes. In fairness, that may shift a bit after this moment, seeing him not as credible candidate.
paragraphs.cn: 第二名大约有50万美元，是一位州参议员。我知道你刚加入讨论，贾勒特，但帮我理解一下为什么他在资金上领先这么多。这意味着人们是捐赠者。有钱人在给他钱。《每日信号》撰稿人贾勒特·斯特普曼：是的。公平地说，在看到他不是一个可信的候选人之后，情况可能会有所改变。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Well, that's a shift by about a million bucks. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，那大约是一百万美元的差距。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: It would -- it would have to be quite a bit.
paragraphs.cn: 那得改变很多才行。

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: And, frankly, it -- Colorado, look, they haven't elected a Republican governor in that state since --
paragraphs.cn: 而且，坦率地说，科罗拉多州，你看，他们自……以来就没有选过共和党州长。

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: So, it's been a long time. I think you sometimes see this in states that have gone one way for a long time. You get sometimes candidates who are not particularly credible. There's a sort of a desperation saying, this guy seems like an outsider. He's, said, look, we saw this in Maine, somebody who's an outsider, in that case actually had a huge amount of success against a political establishment.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，已经很久了。我想你有时会在长期倾向一方的州看到这种情况。有时会出现一些不太可信的候选人。有一种绝望的情绪说，这家伙看起来像个局外人。他说，看，我们在缅因州看到了，一个局外人，实际上在那个案例中，对政治建制派取得了巨大成功。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Can I tell you how far outside --
paragraphs.cn: 我能告诉你他有多局外吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: Just how far outside. MIKE LEON, HOST, "CAN WE PLEASE TALK"
paragraphs.cn: 有多局外。《我们能谈谈吗》主持人迈克·莱昂：

paragraphs.speaker: Stepman
paragraphs.text: A bit outside is probably -- (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: 稍微偏外一点可能是 --  -- （交叉对话）

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: I'm going to -- I needed to be, like, what's happening with this exorcism bit? Is this an internet joke? Is this people sort of, you know, I don't know what I thought. But here's what I saw. VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: 我打算 --  -- 我需要知道，这个驱魔桥段是怎么回事？是网络笑话吗？还是人们有点，你知道，我不知道我怎么想的。但这是我看到的。维克托·马克斯（共和党人），

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Candidate For Governor
paragraphs.text: When you say I perform exorcisms.
paragraphs.cn: 当你说我执行驱魔时。

paragraphs.speaker: Kyle Clark
paragraphs.text: Yes. You described yourself as a, quote/unquote, reluctant exorcist.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。你形容自己是一个，引述，不情愿的驱魔人。

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.text: Yes. Describe to me how your exorcisms work when you command demons out.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。描述一下你的驱魔是如何进行的，当你命令恶魔离开时。

paragraphs.speaker: Marx
paragraphs.text: Well, that's a joke. We identify the demon, like Jesus did. We asked what their name is. And then we give them commands. And they very frequently and typically will answer through the person's mind. They'll tell us what they hear. And then we destroy them, banish them to perfect judgment from Christ.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，这是个玩笑。我们识别恶魔，就像耶稣做的那样。我们问他们的名字。然后我们给他们命令。他们经常且通常会通过人的思想回答。他们会告诉我们他们听到了什么。然后我们摧毁他们，将他们放逐到基督的完美审判中。

paragraphs.speaker: Clark
paragraphs.text: If you're elected governor, would you continue to perform exorcisms while governor?
paragraphs.cn: 如果你当选州长，你会继续在任内进行驱魔吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Marx
paragraphs.text: I would never stop praying for people.
paragraphs.cn: 我永远不会停止为人们祈祷。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: So, Audie, remember when we were talking about candidate quality earlier in the first segment. Shout out to Kyle Clark, the reporter there, for 9News in Denver. I love watching you on social media. He's done a lot on this one.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，奥迪，还记得我们在第一段早些时候讨论候选人素质吗？向丹佛9News的记者凯尔·克拉克致敬。我喜欢在社交媒体上看你。他在这方面做了很多。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.text: He's actually become a bit of a social media star precisely for this coverage.
paragraphs.cn: 他实际上因为这次报道而成为社交媒体明星。

paragraphs.speaker: Leon
paragraphs.text: He has. Precisely for this and the pushback. He's asked different questions of different candidates to give them factual examples. And the fact that he did this, I think it truly exposes a candidate. The fact that he has 2.7, like you said, million dollars in campaign donations. It should be alarming for all of us given this guy's track record and his history and what he just said in that interview there. We're all trying to make sense of exorcisms. And this morning about a candidate, I mean, come on. (CROSS TALK)
